gaps: A snapshot enrichment (or query flag) that inlines the linked opinion cluster's summary/disposition text for opinion-derived docket stubs — for this case the cluster link is the only signal and its content was off-limits
A query filter for era/date (e.g. pre-1925) or docket-number shape, to pull priors genuinely similar to a bare historical stub
stats --group-by disposition buckets the open majority as one '(open)' row; a --resolved-only default hint in the predict prompt would save a step
helpful: fedcourts stats disposition base rates for resolved SCOTUS cases (other 78%, granted 1.4%) anchored the calibration
fedcourts query surfaced resolved 19th-century priors showing merits cases reconcile to disposition 'other'
fedcourts paths resolved the output layout instantly
notes: Bare pre-1925 docket stub; prediction driven by the linked-cluster signal plus corpus base rates. Tooling was sufficient for a calibrated call but not for a confident one.
